decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce the thermal hotspot. I will apply a conservative 1.5x increase to the spreader material conductivity, moving from 264.6 to 396.9 W/(m*K), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and the global bound of 500.0.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly lowering the peak temperature violation.

expected_effects: Flatter temperature profile across the heat spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
